questions.scenario:
A monthly review shows this row:

```text
Resource: asp-devpolaris-orders-prod
Pattern: App Service plan runs all month, even during quiet hours
Known reason: production checkout needs at least one ready runtime
```

Which cost shape is most visible?

options.text: Always-on capacity, because the plan reserves runtime whether traffic is high or low.
Only stored data, because App Service plans charge only for files saved by the app.
Only data movement, because all app cost must come from outbound traffic.
A safety copy, because every running web app is a backup.

questions.explanation: An App Service plan is capacity the team keeps ready. It may be the right choice, but the review should connect that steady cost to traffic, availability, and scaling promises.

questions.scenario:
After release `2026-05-16.2`, Application Insights ingestion rises much faster than request traffic. Normal checkout now writes this message on every request:

```text
DEBUG route=POST /orders payloadBytes=18421 message=request body accepted
```

Which cost shape should the team discuss first?

options.text: Usage-based work, because noisy telemetry creates more ingestion and processing around normal traffic.
Safety copies, because every debug payload is a restore point.
Data movement only, because log ingestion is always internet egress.
Always-on SQL capacity, because every log line resizes the database tier.

questions.explanation: Usage-based cost can spike when the application does extra work. A verbose debug line can multiply observability cost without adding enough diagnostic value.

questions.scenario: Blob versioning is enabled for receipt files. Storage cost grows steadily even though the number of current receipts looks normal.

options.text: Stored data and safety copies, because old versions are retained to support recovery.
Only idle compute, because old blob versions are running servers.
Only database capacity, because Blob Storage cannot keep old object state.
Only budget notification cost, because versions are alert messages.

questions.explanation: Blob versions are retained data. They may be useful protection against overwrite or delete, but they still consume storage and need a retention policy.

questions.scenario: Which cost-shape pairings are reasonable for `devpolaris-orders-api`?

options.text: Stored data: Azure SQL size, Blob receipts, retained logs, and backup storage grow over time.
Safety copies: backups, soft delete, blob versions, and restore targets kept for recovery.
Data movement: cross-region replication or outbound traffic can add cost to the service path.
Always-on capacity: a deleted test database that no one can identify is automatically a healthy production promise.

questions.explanation: Stored data, safety copies, and data movement are real Azure cost shapes. Unowned leftovers can cost money too, but ownership and purpose are missing, so they are not a healthy example of intentional capacity.
